metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Outlaws of Thunder Junction emphasizes value, removal, and fatties with a slower format than its predecessor. It avoids the unbeatable bomb rares and noninteractive races that plagued Murders at Karlov Manor, offering more time for synergy assembly and color-splashing opportunities.

color_notes.{G} Green: Best color. Exceptional mana-fixing through ramp, premium fatties (Spinewoods Paladin, Cactarantula, Giant Beaver), strong removal (Throw from the Saddle), and splashing utility (Snakeskin Veil). Often overdrafted.
color_notes.{W} White: Efficient early creatures (Trained Arynx, Sterling Keykeeper, Holy Cow), premium removal (Mystical Tether), and excellent tricks (Take Up the Shield). Creature sizing trails green.
color_notes.{B} Black: Best common removal (Consuming Ashes, Desert's Due), efficient early plays (Vault Plunderer, Desperate Bloodseeker), and strong value cards (Mourner's Surprise, Ambush Gigapede). Limited creature sizing.
color_notes.{R} Red: Best common is Explosive Derailment. Secondary removal (Prickly Pair) and go-wide payoff (Outlaws' Fury). Often outmatched by value decks.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Weakest. Playable counterspell (Phantom Interference) and Loan Shark value, but the lowest-quality common creatures in the format.

key_gameplay_notes: Green's strength can lead to scarcity mid-draft — be ready to pivot.
Splashing is encouraged through desert mana-fixing.
Board-wipes are uncommon; most removal is targeted.
Deserts outperform expectations thanks to mana-fixing and crime synergy.

mechanics.name: Crime
mechanics.summary: Flexible mechanic rewarding both dedicated and incidental synergies. Targeting opponents' permanents and players counts as committing a crime.
mechanics.performance: Easy to enable; payoffs scale with density.

mechanics.name: Plot
mechanics.summary: Cast plotted spells at a discount on a later turn. Interesting decision-making; strong both as a standalone payoff and as a synergy engine.
mechanics.performance: Format-defining — enables tempo and value plays.

mechanics.name: Saddle
mechanics.summary: Less binary than vehicle mechanics — saddling grants powerful but temporary bonuses. Core to GW Mounts.
mechanics.performance: Top-tier when paired with mount creatures.

mechanics.name: Spree
mechanics.summary: Standard modular spell design with selectable add-on modes. Limited impact on format shape.

mechanics.name: Outlaws / Mercenaries / Deserts
mechanics.summary: Outlaws are slightly underpowered; mercenaries are useful across RW/BR/WB; deserts outperform expectations due to mana-fixing and crime synergy.
mechanics.performance: Deserts are the most impactful subtype.

tier_1.name: GW Mounts (Midrange/Aggro)
tier_1.strategy: Every mount creature performs above average. Synergy with removal creates an efficiency advantage.

tier_1.name: BG Recursion (Midrange/Control)
tier_1.strategy: Best removal suite plus mana-fixing enables greedy splashing. Graveyard payoffs reward creature-heavy decks.

tier_1.name: WB Attrition (Midrange)
tier_1.strategy: Premium removal pairs with efficient creatures; sacrifice outlets enable value generation.

tier_2.name: RG Ferocious (Midrange/Aggro)
tier_2.strategy: Rewards 4+ power creatures with powerful payoffs. Green dominates creature quality.

tier_2.name: RW Mercenaries (Aggro)
tier_2.strategy: Go-wide token strategy leveraging mercenary generation and board-based payoffs.

tier_2.name: GU Ramp Plot (Midrange/Control)
tier_2.strategy: Plotting creatures at a discount sets up powerful future turns. Splashing-friendly.

tier_2.name: UB Crime (Control)
tier_2.strategy: Removal-focused with crime payoffs; tempo-oriented crime suite.

tier_2.name: BR Outlaws (Aggro/Midrange)
tier_2.strategy: Outlaw synergies with creature-based removal and token generation.

tier_3.name: WU Flash Plot (Tempo/Midrange)
tier_3.strategy: Flash creatures with plot synergy provide tempo advantage but lack density of premium payoffs.

tier_3.name: UR Two-Spells Plot (Control)
tier_3.strategy: "No spells cast" and "two spells cast" payoffs create inconsistent deck building pressures.

{W} White.name: Inventive Wingsmith
{W} White.note: Outclassed by Holy Cow and Vengeful Townsfolk.

general_tips: Splash via desert mana-fixing — the set rewards greedy mana bases.
Graveyard synergies reward patient play; let trades happen.
No graveyard hate is available — feel free to commit to recursion strategies.
Pivot away from green if it gets cut early; the rest of the format has playable decks.
Avoid forcing WU Flash Plot or UR Two-Spells Plot without strong signals — both are inconsistent.
